steps.title: Compare the ancestry percentages
steps.content: Fan's team concluded that modern alpacas get only 64 percent of their genetic material from their wild ancestor, the vicuña, while llamas get 95.5 percent from their wild ancestor, the guanaco. The rest of each genome comes from later interbreeding between llamas and alpacas.

steps.title: Draw the supported inference
steps.content: Choice A adds unsupported claims about genetic diversity. Choice B incorrectly ties llama genetic material to alpaca domestication, even though the interbreeding happened much later. Choice D invents ancient interbreeding among wild populations. The percentage comparison supports a larger wild-ancestor difference for alpacas than for llamas. The answer is C.
